perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall not include information which recipient is able to prove by documentary evidence (a) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available by publication, commercial or otherwise, through no fault of the Receiving Party, (b) was known by the recipient at the time of the disclosure or was independently developed by the Receiving Party without the use of any Confidential Information, or (c) must be disclosed pursuant to applicable legal disclosure requirements or legal process.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information shall not include information which recipient is able to prove by documentary evidence (a) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available by publication, commercial or otherwise, through no fault of the Receiving Party, (b) was known by the recipient at the time of the disclosure or was independently developed by the Receiving Party without the use of any Protected Information, or (c) must be disclosed pursuant to applicable legal disclosure requirements or legal process.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Information' in Section 2(b) conflicts with the definition in Section 1 and the rest of the document. Because Section 1 defined only 'Confidential Information', and Section 2 now includes both, there's an ambiguity whether the exceptions to disclosure apply uniformly to all 'Protected Information' and 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees that all Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and no license or other rights in the Confidential Information is granted hereby. The Receiving Party will not disclose the existence, content and/or substance of any of the Confidential Information to any third party; nor develop, manufacture, produce and/or distribute any software product(s) derived from or which otherwise use any of the Confidential Information. The Disclosing Party agrees to return (and have any third party to who it supplied the Confidential Information return) to the Receiving Party, immediately upon notification by the Disclosing Party in writing, all Confidential Information, including but not limited to all computer programs, documentation, notes, plans, drawings, and copies or reproductions thereof, in any form or medium whatsoever, and not to retain any copies thereof.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Information' in the exception clause. The contradicted text refers specifically to 'Confidential Information', outlining the obligations related to its use and return. Because Section 1 defined only 'Confidential Information', and Section 2 now includes both 'Confidential Information' and 'Protected Information', there is ambiguity whether the obligations outlined in the contradicted text apply to 'Protected Information'. This creates an in-text contradiction as it is not clear if the terms apply interchangeably, thus conflicting with the definitions of Misaligned Terminology, leading to an internal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party expressly agrees not to use the Confidential Information for purposes other than those necessary to consider whether to enter into a Transaction and shall strictly limit its disclosure to such of its employees, directors and advisors having a need to know such information, which parties shall be advised that such information is Confidential Information and subject to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party expressly agrees not to use the Private Information for purposes other than those necessary to consider whether to enter into a Transaction and shall strictly limit its disclosure to such of its employees, directors and advisors having a need to know such information, which parties shall be advised that such information is Confidential Information and subject to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Information' creates a conflict. The receiving party has now the obligation to not use private information, but the term is not defined anywhere in the document. Causing a discrepancy to all furhter sections where the term 'Confidential Information' is used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall immediately notify the Disclosing Party in writing of any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information or the Negotiations, and shall provide a detailed description of the circumstances of the disclosure and the parties involved, and shall cooperate in any reasonable efforts to limit or respond to such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information,' which is assumed to be defined elsewhere in the contract. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Private Information,' which, according to the explanation, is not defined in the document. The contradicted text references 'Confidential Information.' Therefore, because the changed text now imposes an obligation on 'Private Information,' it contradicts the contradicted section, which pertains to 'Confidential Information' and creates a misalignment of terminology causing a strong in-text contradiction as per the provided definition.

perturbation.original_text: Except as must be disclosed pursuant to applicable legal disclosure requirements or legal process, neither party nor any of its respective representatives may, without the prior written consent of the other party, disclose to any person (other than to its employees, directors and advisors having a need to know such information) that the parties have exchanged confidential information, are engaged in negotiations for a Transaction, or the proposed terms of a Transaction (collectively, the "Negotiations") until such information has been disclosed by an appropriate legal filing and/or press release mutually agreed upon by SER and EIS.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as must be disclosed pursuant to applicable legal disclosure requirements or legal process, neither party nor any of its respective representatives may, without the prior written consent of the other party, disclose to any person (other than to its employees, directors and advisors having a need to know such information) that the parties have exchanged private information, are engaged in negotiations for a Transaction, or the proposed terms of a Transaction (collectively, the "Negotiations") until such information has been disclosed by an appropriate legal filing and/or press release mutually agreed upon by SER and EIS.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'confidential information' to 'private information' in Section 4 creates a contradiction with previous clauses where 'Confidential Information' is used to define what information should remain undisclosed. Now that one term is used, and it is not defined, it will create a confusion in the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party hereby acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information or the Negotiations could cause irreparable harm and significant injury, the extent of which may be difficult to ascertain. Accordingly, each party agrees that each party shall have the right to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief from breaches of this Agreement, in addition to any other rights and remedies each party may have.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'confidential information' with 'private information'. The contradicted text refers explicitly to 'Confidential Information'. Since 'private information' is not defined, and the contract relies heavily on the term 'Confidential Information' for determining what information should not be disclosed, the change creates a contradiction because it introduces ambiguity about whether the contradicted text still applies to the same set of information. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology definition, as the change creates a confusion about which terms should be enforced.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will not disclose the existence, content and/or substance of any of the Confidential Information to any third party; nor develop, manufacture, produce and/or distribute any software product(s) derived from or which otherwise use any of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party will not disclose the existence, content and/or substance of any of the Secured Information to any third party; nor develop, manufacture, produce and/or distribute any software product(s) derived from or which otherwise use any of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Secured Information' creates a conflict. Since 'Secured Information' is not defined, its scope is unclear, potentially leading to disputes over what information the Receiving Party is prohibited from disclosing. Since it only changed the one instance within the text, that leaves the rest of the document, still refferring to confidential information, which it does not relate to the change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties agree that any product, plan, strategy or other thing or concept jointly developed as a result of, or during, the Negotiations, shall be the joint property of both parties hereto. Neither party shall use, license or otherwise exploit such jointly developed item(s) without the express prior written consent of the other party hereto, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed. Notwithstanding the above, any product, plan, strategy or other thing or concept that a party can prove was known by the recipient at the time of the development with the other party or was independently developed shall not be considered a jointly developed item(s).
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Secured Information' creates a contradiction because 'Secured Information' is not defined, leading to uncertainty about its scope. The contradicted text refers to jointly developed items resulting from negotiations, implying a shared understanding of 'Confidential Information'. By changing it to 'Secured Information' in the changed text, the definition is unclear, making it difficult to determine if the jointly developed items are subject to the confidentiality restrictions described in the original text. This misalignment in terminology creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party hereby acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information or the Negotiations could cause irreparable harm and significant injury, the extent of which may be difficult to ascertain. Accordingly, each party agrees that each party shall have the right to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief from breaches of this Agreement, in addition to any other rights and remedies each party may have.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party hereby acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure or use of Protected Information or the Negotiations could cause irreparable harm and significant injury, the extent of which may be difficult to ascertain. Accordingly, each party agrees that each party shall have the right to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief from breaches of this Agreement, in addition to any other rights and remedies each party may have.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' in Section 10, where this term has not been previously defined, leaves to doubts of the definition of it in this section. The document started out with Confidential Information which had some kind of defition, but the document did not define this Protected Information, nor how it is used. This leads to ambiguity and contradiction in the whole text, leaving to doubts of what the extent of it is.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees to indemnify and hold the Disclosing Party harmless from and against all claims, losses, liabilities, damages, expenses and costs (including, without limitation, reasonable fees for attorneys, expert witnesses and court costs) which result from a breach or threatened breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' without defining 'Protected Information' creates ambiguity. The Contradicted Text refers to 'a breach or threatened breach of this Agreement'. Since the Agreement now uses an undefined term ('Protected Information'), it's unclear if a breach related to 'Protected Information' triggers the indemnity clause. This creates a contradiction because the scope of the indemnity is now uncertain due to the undefined term, creating a strong in-text contradiction, as per the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition.
